commands.review_summary:
The PR adds default-agent generated plugin model catalog inheritance for secondary-agent model discovery, threads the inherited agent dir through cached discovery, fingerprints inherited catalogs, and adds ModelRegistry tests.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +79. Total +151 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-main source shows plugin catalog rows are only loaded from the active agent dir, and the PR supplies live before/after terminal proof for a secondary Google/Gemini agent with a model-less local catalog.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocker is a narrow code/test repair in ModelRegistry that automation can attempt without choosing new product semantics.

target.title: fix(agents): inherit default agent model catalog for secondary agents
target.body:
# fix(agents): inherit default agent model catalog for secondary agents

## What & why

Secondary (non-default) agents created with `openclaw agents add` fail at runtime
with `FailoverError: Unknown model: google/gemini-2.5-flash` when using a
Google/Gemini model authenticated via `GEMINI_API_KEY`. The default/`main` agent
works with the same model and key.

**Root cause:** model discovery only runs for the default agent at gateway
startup, so generated plugin model catalogs (`plugins/<provider>/catalog.json`)
are only populated under the default agent dir. A secondary agent gets an
apiKey-only google catalog with **no models**. `ModelRegistry.loadCustomModels()`
loads plugin catalogs only from the current agent dir, with no read-through to
the default agent — unlike auth profiles, which already inherit via
`inheritedAuthDir`.

## The fix

Add read-through inheritance for generated plugin model catalogs, mirroring the
existing auth-profile read-through:

- `ModelRegistry` accepts an optional `inheritedAgentDir`. When set (and distinct
  from the agent's own dir), its generated plugin catalogs are loaded **first**
  (so any provider request config they register is overridden by the local
  agent's own catalogs — local always wins), and their models are merged as a
  **gap-fill only**: a `(provider, id)` pair already defined locally is never
  overridden.
- A broken/invalid inherited catalog is skipped, never breaking the local agent.
- `discoverModels` threads `inheritedAgentDir` through to the registry.
- `discoverCachedAgentStores` passes the resolved default agent dir, and the
  inherited catalog files are folded into the discovery-cache fingerprint so
  changes to the default agent's catalog invalidate the secondary agent's cached
  snapshot.

The default agent never inherits from itself (guarded by a path-equality check),
so its behavior is unchanged.

## Related

Related to (but a distinct root cause from) #58289 / PR #58373. That issue covers
a secondary agent **missing `models.json` entirely** when the provider plan is
empty, and its fix copies `main`'s `models.json` as a one-time bootstrap. This PR
addresses the case where the catalog **exists but is generated apiKey-only with no
models**, and fixes it with read-through catalog inheritance in `ModelRegistry`
(mirroring the existing auth-profile inheritance) rather than a file copy.

## Files

- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts` — `inheritedAgentDir` option + read-through merge
- `src/agents/agent-model-discovery.ts` — thread option through `discoverModels`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model-discovery-cache.ts` — pass default dir + fingerprint
- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts` — 3 new tests

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A secondary (non-default) agent created with
  `openclaw agents add` failed on every turn with `FailoverError: Unknown model:
  google/gemini-2.5-flash`, while the default/`main` agent resolved the exact same
  model and `GEMINI_API_KEY` fine. Root cause is the secondary agent's generated
  google catalog being written apiKey-only with no model rows, and `ModelRegistry`
  having no read-through to the default agent's catalog.

- Real environment tested: My own OpenClaw 2026.6.2 install on macOS (local
  gateway under launchd). Real on-disk agent dirs `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent`
  (default) and `~/.openclaw/agents/english-tutor/agent` (secondary), Google
  Gemini provider via `GEMINI_API_KEY`, default model `google/gemini-2.5-flash`.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Applied the patch to my real
  install, `pnpm build`, restarted the gateway, reset the secondary agent's google
  catalog back to the apiKey-only state that `agents add` leaves behind, then ran a
  real turn through the gateway (the same lane a Telegram DM uses):
  ```
  $ cat ~/.openclaw/agents/english-tutor/agent/plugins/google/catalog.json
  {"generatedBy":"openclaw-plugin-model-catalog-v1","providers":{"google":{"apiKey":"GEMINI_API_KEY"}}}   # no models

  $ openclaw agent --agent english-tutor -m "Hi Lexi, correct this: I has two cats yesterday." --json
  ```
  I also exercised the patched discovery path (`discoverModels`) directly against
  the real `english-tutor` and `main` agent dirs, with the secondary catalog reset
  to apiKey-only.

- Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output.

  End-to-end through the patched gateway (secondary catalog still model-less on disk):
  ```
  $ openclaw agent --agent english-tutor -m "Hi Lexi, correct this: I has two cats yesterday." --json
  "status": "ok"
  "text": "...Câu đúng phải là: \"I **had** two cats yesterday.\" ..."   # real reply, model resolved
  ```
  Patched `discoverModels` against the real on-disk dirs:
  ```
  secondary agent dir : ~/.openclaw/agents/english-tutor/agent   (catalog: apiKey-only, no models)
  BEFORE (no inheritance): Unknown model (undefined)  <-- the bug
  AFTER  (inherit main)  : google/gemini-2.5-flash  api=google-generative-ai  baseUrl=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta  <-- resolved
  ```
  Before-fix live failure (OpenClaw 2026.6.2, same install, before the patch):
  ```
  $ openclaw agent --agent english-tutor -m "hello" --json
  GatewayClientRequestError: FailoverError: Unknown model: google/gemini-2.5-flash
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The secondary agent resolves
  `google/gemini-2.5-flash` and returns a real `"status": "ok"` reply through the
  gateway, with **no manual catalog seeding**. The inherited model resolves through
  the native `google-generative-ai` transport (`baseUrl
  https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta`), confirming correct provider
  routing. The same turn returned `Unknown model: google/gemini-2.5-flash` before
  the patch.

- What was not tested: Only verified with the Google/Gemini provider via
  `GEMINI_API_KEY` on macOS; other providers and OS platforms not exercised live.
  Verified end-to-end through the CLI/gateway lane but not through an actual
  Telegram client.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Logs are from my personal install;
  API key value is redacted (only the `GEMINI_API_KEY` ref name appears). The
  before-fix capture and after-fix capture are from the same machine with the patch
  toggled on/off, not a clean-room CI environment.

- Before evidence: see the before-fix failure output included under "Evidence after
  fix" above.

### Supplemental (build / typecheck / automated tests — not a substitute for the above)

```
$ pnpm build        # exit 0
$ pnpm tsgo:all     # full prod + test typecheck, all projects — exit 0, 0 errors
$ npx oxlint <4 touched files>   # no warnings/errors
$ npx vitest run src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts \
      src/agents/agent-model-discovery*.test.ts            # 36 passed (10 files)
```
New tests cover: (1) secondary agent inherits the default agent's catalog models,
(2) no inheritance without `inheritedAgentDir` (reproduces the bug), (3) the
secondary agent's own catalog wins over inherited models.

### Known unrelated / pre-existing
- A full `pnpm test` run has 4 shards each with 1 failing test, all unrelated to
  this change (none reference `ModelRegistry`/`discoverModels`); they are macOS
  `/private/var` path-normalization / packaged-worker / fixture assertions:
  `src/agents/compaction-planning-worker.test.ts`,
  `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`,
  `src/commands/agent.session.test.ts`,
  `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-shadow-trial.test.ts`.
- `pnpm check`'s `npm-shrinkwrap` guard reports a stale shrinkwrap; pre-existing on
  `main` and unrelated (this change touches no dependencies). The remaining `pnpm
  check` steps (typecheck via `tsgo:all`, oxlint) pass.

## AI assistance
Drafted with Claude Code (Opus). Reviewed and validated by me. Session log
available on request.
